text: Patient presents for a general adult health exam; has not seen a physician since college
transcript_quote: It says you booked a general exam... I haven't really been to a doctor since college.

text: Chronic low back pain for several years, originating from a lifting injury during college

slots.onset: lifting injury in junior year of college

transcript_quote: It's the same low back thing I've had for a few years. Junior year I helped basically my roommate's entire floor move out in one day... Felt something pull.

text: Current low back pain described as a dull band across the very bottom of the back, rated 4/10
transcript_quote: A dull band across the very bottom of my back. Right now it's like a four out of ten.

text: Back pain worsened by prolonged sitting/desk work (up to 10 hours during software release crunch)
transcript_quote: Desk days make it worse... ten hours in a chair, and by Friday I'm walking like a question mark.

text: Back pain relieved by walking, hot showers, and ibuprofen
transcript_quote: Walking. Hot showers. I had ibuprofen for a while, which worked

text: No radiation of pain down the leg, no numbness, tingling, or weakness in the feet
transcript_quote: No, nothing like that. It stays in the back.

text: No bladder or bowel dysfunction, no fevers, no night pain, no weight loss
transcript_quote: No. It's boring pain. Reliable, boring pain.

text: Past surgical history: appendectomy at age 15
transcript_quote: You had your appendix out as a teenager, right? PT: Yeah, when I was fifteen. That's my only surgery.

text: No current medications
transcript_quote: Nothing. The empty ibuprofen bottle doesn't count, I assume.

text: No known allergies
transcript_quote: Allergies? PT: None.

text: Never smoked tobacco; never vaped
transcript_quote: Never. Neither.

text: Occasional alcohol use — a beer with roommates on weekends
transcript_quote: Occasionally. A beer with the roommates on weekends, if that.

text: PHQ-2 screen positive with total score 5
PHQ-9 total score 17, moderately severe depressive symptoms
transcript_quote: you score a 17, which lands in the moderately severe range for depressive symptoms.

text: Reports low interest/pleasure most days, depressed/hopeless mood most days, difficulty falling asleep nearly every night, fatigue nearly every day, poor appetite (skips lunch more than half the time), feelings of failure several days, trouble concentrating more than half the days, and restlessness several days
transcript_quote: Yeah. Most days, kind of a gray filter over everything.

text: Denies suicidal ideation or thoughts of self-harm
transcript_quote: No. Never that. I want to be really clear on that one — no.

text: Depressive symptoms present since winter, associated with work crunch, girlfriend moving away for grad school, isolation, and poor sleep
transcript_quote: Since winter, maybe? Work went into crunch... my girlfriend moved across the country for grad school in January, so evenings got really quiet.

text: HARK domestic violence/abuse screen negative, total score 0
transcript_quote: Good — that screen is a zero.

text: DAST-10 drug screen score 1 (low); reports using a cannabis gummy twice this winter for back pain/sleep
transcript_quote: A friend gave me a gummy once this winter when my back was bad and I couldn't sleep. Twice, total... that puts you at a one out of ten on the drug screen

text: Back exam: low back muscles tight bilaterally, spine non-tender, negative straight leg raise, normal strength and reflexes — muscular/mechanical picture

slots.finding: bilateral paraspinal tightness, non-tender spine, negative SLR, normal strength/reflexes

transcript_quote: Your low back muscles are tight on both sides, but the spine itself isn't tender, leg raises don't reproduce anything, strength and reflexes are all normal. That's a muscular, mechanical picture

text: Blood pressure elevated at 136/94 mmHg; to be rechecked at follow-up rather than treated today
transcript_quote: your blood pressure today is 136 over 94, which is higher than I want to see at twenty-two... we'll recheck at the four-week visit rather than react today.

text: Family history of hypertension (father)
transcript_quote: My dad has blood pressure stuff, so... noted.

text: Heart rate 89 /min
transcript_quote: Heart rate's 89

text: Respiratory rate 15 /min
Weight 77 kg, height 185.2 cm, BMI 22.5 (normal)
transcript_quote: at 77 kilos your BMI is 22.5, dead normal.

text: Oral exam shows red, puffy gums along edges that bleed with flossing — consistent with gingivitis
transcript_quote: Your gums are red and puffy along the edges, and I'd bet they bleed when you floss... Classic gingivitis loop.

text: Prescribed ibuprofen 400 mg oral tablet, take one with food when back pain flares (every 4-6 hours as needed)
transcript_quote: I'm prescribing ibuprofen 400 milligrams — take one with food when it flares, not on an empty stomach.

text: Counseled on back care: stand/move out of chair every hour, walk at lunch, hinge at hips and keep load close when lifting
transcript_quote: out of the chair every hour, even five minutes. Walk at lunch. When you lift — hinge at the hips, keep the load close.

text: Counseled on depression self-management: fixed wake time, morning daylight, daily movement, and scheduling calls with girlfriend
transcript_quote: a fixed wake time even after bad nights, daylight in the morning, movement every day... schedule the girlfriend calls like meetings

text: Offered referral to counseling/therapy with insurance-compatible list including evening telehealth options; patient to review list
transcript_quote: counseling — a therapist is the best-studied tool for exactly this picture. I can give you a list that works with your insurance, including a couple that do evening telehealth.

text: Dental referral placed for gingivitis (cleaning and daily flossing recommended)
transcript_quote: I'm sending a dental referral today.

text: Counseled on gingivitis: reverses with dental cleaning and daily flossing; bleeding resolves in a couple of weeks
transcript_quote: It reverses with a real cleaning and daily flossing — the bleeding stops once the gums calm down, usually a couple of weeks.

text: Follow-up in 4 weeks to recheck back, blood pressure, and re-score depression (PHQ-9)
transcript_quote: Front desk will book the four-week recheck — back, blood pressure, and mood score, all three.

text: Safety counseling: contact clinic same-day or use crisis line if depressive symptoms worsen or suicidal thoughts arise
transcript_quote: if the gray filter ever gets darker than what you told me today... you call us same-day or use the crisis line on the sheet.

text: Medication reconciliation completed

PRAPARE assessment: full-time employment, private insurance, stable housing (3 people at address), education more than high school, feels safe at home, close contact with people 3-5 times/week, annual household income ~$91,796, English preferred language
Work-related stress reported in context of software release crunch
transcript_quote: Work went into crunch — we shipped two releases back to back

text: Depression screening procedures and PHQ-9 assessment performed
Domestic abuse screening performed
Substance use / drug abuse screening performed
